verdict.label: Worth checking
verdict.headline: Supertubos Lunch
verdict.reason: Supertubos is worth a quick check for lunch, but it is not a guaranteed session.  Treat it as a check-first call rather than a sure thing.  The wind should help keep it tidy.  The tide is lining up for a powerful wedge, so keep the comfort level in mind.

conversationalSummary.text: TODAY I’d head out for Supertubos around lunch, but only after a quick camera check before leaving since Surfline consistency looks a bit “lull-y” and the spot surf may be on the small side. The wind direction seems to line up nicely, and the tide rising with a push should help the barrel/wedge type setup, especially mid-tide. If the cams don’t show a clear step-up and clean lines, I’d skip it and save the drive.

wetsuit.reason: 4/3 remains the safer default for Peniche unless water is warm and wind is light.

best.explanation: Supertubos looks like a clean small window for Worth checking, High confidence: 0.3-0.6 m, 49 kJ, 11 s period, lully 7/100, and good wind at 20 km/h.  A fast, hollow sand-bottom wave that gets serious quickly; this is best for short, punchy bodyboard barrels when it is small enough for comfort; 10-13s at mid tide is the prime barrel / wedge setup for Supertubos / Molhe Leste.  Watch: Surfline consistency is lully; spot surf looks too small.
best.shortExplanation: Supertubos is worth a quick check for lunch, but it is not a guaranteed session.  Treat it as a check-first call rather than a sure thing.  The wind should help keep it tidy.  The tide is lining up for a powerful wedge, so keep the comfort level in mind.

photo.alt: Photo of Cantinho da Baia surf spot

wetsuit.reason: 4/3 remains the safer default for Peniche unless water is warm and wind is light.

ranked.explanation: Cantinho da Baia looks like a fun-sized window for Safe fun, Medium confidence: 0.9-1.2 m, 433 kJ, 10 s period, consistent 71/100, and wind is the main risk.  A forgiving sandy bay with lots of take-off points; this is best for easy wave count and cleaner faces when the north wind is bothering exposed beaches; 10-13s at mid tide gives the sheltered bay workable wrap-around lines.  Watch: wind angle is poor for this spot; gusts over 35 km/h could make it messy.
ranked.shortExplanation: Cantinho da Baia is worth a quick check for after work (18:00 start), but it is not a guaranteed session.  Treat it as a check-first call rather than a sure thing.  Wind is the main thing to watch.  The tide should help the bay stay organised.
warnings: wind angle is poor for this spot
gusts over 35 km/h could make it messy

ranked.explanation: Cerro looks like a solid window for Worth checking, Medium confidence: 1.2-1.5 m, 283 kJ, 10 s period, consistent 69/100, and wind is the main risk.  A sheltered beachbreak with gentle lefts and rights, somewhat similar to Cantinho but less consistent; this is best for manageable bodyboard waves when north-northwest swell reaches the bay with clean southerly wind; 10-13s at mid tide works well on exposed beaches.  Watch: wind angle is poor for this spot; gusts over 35 km/h could make it messy.
ranked.shortExplanation: Cerro is worth a quick check for after work (18:00 start), but it is not a guaranteed session.  Treat it as a check-first call rather than a sure thing.  Wind is the main thing to watch.  The tide and timing are lining up well for this spot.
warnings: wind angle is poor for this spot

ranked.explanation: Baleal looks like a fun-sized window for Safe fun, Low confidence: 0.6-1.1 m, 218 kJ, 8 s period, consistent 68/100, and wind is the main risk.  A broad, forgiving beach setup with several peaks; this is best for spreading out and finding a manageable wave when the bay has enough push.  Watch: wind angle is poor for this spot; 6-9s at mid tide is soft and sectiony on the Baleal sandbanks.
ranked.shortExplanation: Baleal is worth a quick check for after work (18:00 start), but it is not a guaranteed session.  Treat it as a check-first call rather than a sure thing.  Wind is the main thing to watch.  The tide is part of the picture, but not the deciding factor.
warnings: wind angle is poor for this spot
6-9s at mid tide is soft and sectiony on the Baleal sandbanks
